intro: Prismatomeris borneensis is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, a group of flowering plants commonly known as madder or bedstraw. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Prismatomeris borneensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
